original_hypothesis.title: Factory registry can be poisoned with arbitrary pools (addPool has no validation), enabling UI/indexer deception and misrouting funds
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Factory.addPool allows the owner to register any `pool` address under arbitrary base/quote/creator keys without verifying it is a MagicLP clone with matching tokens/parameters. If the owner (or compromised owner key) registers malicious pools, integrators relying on the registry may route liquidity or swaps to attacker-controlled contracts.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While owner compromise is low-realism under the model, the absence of validation is a concrete code defect that amplifies the blast radius of an admin mistake or compromise, and can cause user fund loss via trusted registry consumption.
deployment_assumptions: Integrators/UI/indexers treat Factory.pools as authoritative and auto-route users
Factory owner registers an invalid/malicious pool (by mistake or malicious intent)
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Factory.sol addPool explicitly states it doesn't check validity; no deployment-time enforcement found.

proof_of_signal.pattern: addPool note: 'doesn't check if the pool is valid or if it's already registered'

preconditions: Downstream systems use pools[base][quote] or userPools[creator] to select trading venues

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: registry can contain non-MagicLP pools, enabling spoofing/misrouting
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no check that pool.implementation == implementation, no check of _BASE_TOKEN_/_QUOTE_TOKEN_ matches

analysis_questions: Do official frontends/indexers rely solely on Factory registry for path discovery?
Is there an on-chain method to validate a pool (e.g., IMagicLP(pool)._BASE_TOKEN_()) and should Factory enforce it?
Could a malicious pool mimic IMagicLP interface and steal funds via Router path usage?
validation_requirements: Threat-model integration: how pools are discovered in production
Prototype a fake pool registered via addPool and evaluate Router/user losses if UI uses it

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Compromised owner registers a malicious pool as the canonical pool for a popular pair. UI routes users to it, users transfer funds, malicious contract steals deposits/swaps.
prerequisites: Owner compromise or malicious owner action

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Potential direct user fund theft if routing is registry-based
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Registry can be corrected, but stolen funds unrecoverable

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No tests ensuring Factory registry contains only valid MagicLP clones

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: monitor addPool events and validate pool bytecode / immutables off-chain

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
